title: Tangy Juicy Cantonese Pork Belly Braise
description: A classic Cantonese dish featuring slow-cooked pork belly in a sweet and tangy sauce, perfect for a comforting dinner.

ingredients.name: Pork belly, skin removed
ingredients.notes: Ask your butcher to remove the skin

ingredients.name: Clove
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the number of cloves based on your personal preference

ingredients.name: Light soy sauce
ingredients.notes: You can substitute with regular soy sauce if light soy sauce is not available

ingredients.name: Oyster sauce
ingredients.notes: Optional, but adds a rich flavor

ingredients.name: Rice vinegar
ingredients.notes: You can use white vinegar as a substitute

ingredients.name: Brown sugar
ingredients.notes: Optional, but adds a touch of sweetness

ingredients.name: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Adjust the number of garlic cloves based on your personal preference

ingredients.name: Ginger, sliced
ingredients.notes: Adjust the size of the ginger slice based on your personal preference

instructions: In a large Dutch oven or heavy pot, combine the pork belly, soy sauce, oyster sauce (if using), rice vinegar, sugar, garlic, and ginger.
Braise the pork belly over low heat for 2 hours, or until it's tender and slightly caramelized.
After 2 hours, remove the pork belly from the pot and let it rest for 10 minutes.
Using two forks, shred the pork belly into bite-sized pieces.
Strain the braising liquid and discard the solids.
Return the pork belly to the pot and add the strained braising liquid.
Simmer the pork belly over low heat for another 10-15 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the pork is nicely coated.
Taste and adjust the seasoning with salt and sugar as needed.
Garnish with chopped scallions and serve with steamed rice or noodles.
